True to its name, not a sliver of light got through.

"If we mess up, you are punished to come stand in here." Rong Huai stuffed his hands in his pockets and continued coyly, "Of course, if you don't make a mistake, you might end up in here too~"

Aside from the dark room, there were also weighted sandbags that were not used for exercise but for… punishment.

In the teachers' offices, the standard equipment seemed to be an electric baton, similar to the one the security guard had used.

There were so many.

The list went on.

Rong Huai continued his cynical mockery, which had been livestreamed fully. The results were shocking.

——[Is this a school? Or a prison??]

——[F*ck, are these people human? Running five kilometers as a standard, and washing people with cold water during winter?!]

——[They confiscate all personal belongings upon entering, even cell phones are handed to teachers… Even the text messages responding to their parents are made up by the teachers? So the students have zero privacy?!]

The principal continued to quibble. "We're following the examples of foreign elite private schools by implementing military management techniques and gentlemanly education. All the students who graduate from here are very outstanding…"

Shi Sui couldn't help but retort coldly. "What about those who don't graduate?"

The principal choked.

Those who couldn't bear the abuse over several years would be both physically and psychologically scarred for the rest of their lives.

"This… This only means students are too emotionally fragile. Nowadays, children are too s.e.n.s.i.t.i.v.e and vain, which is why they are so addicted to playing games and their phones all the time. Our school aids them to get back on the right track for their life ahead…"

"Oh, shut up you!" Li Jianyu sputtered. "Can you be a human for two seconds? Do you know how much pain these children must be feeling being subjected to your torture everyday? Don't you think their parents will be upset if they knew what you were doing?!"

The atmosphere became silent.

Li Jianyu realized something as he suddenly turned to Rong Huai and the other teenagers.

It seemed there was finally emotion in their blank expressions.

Only, it wasn't joy.

The corners of their mouths twisted helplessly with bitter resent.

Li Jianyu felt his scalp tingle with numbness. He didn't dare believe it, but he also had to believe it…

The comment section exploded.

——[As long as there is demand, there will be a supply. Let that sink in]

——[My god, how hopeless must these kids be…]

——[They don't want to spend time with their kids, but they'll spend money for other people to beat their own kids??]

——[So the parents all know? They knew full well that they were sending their kids into a school that used physical punishment?!]

In the gloomy atmosphere, the girl's sweet voice sounded like an otherworldly being descended from heaven:

"It's almost lunch time. Kids, are you hungry?"

chapters.title: Chapter 205 - I Get Embarrassed Too
chapters.body:
Chapter 205 - I Get Embarrassed Too

Rong Huai and the other boys were stunned.

This question had caught them off guard.

It seems like nobody really cared about them, whether it was their families or the school…

They had been children labelled by shame; because of their 'rebelliousness', 'disobedience', 'obsession with the internet', they were sent here for strict discipline.

Aside from this cage in their lives, there wasn't even a place they could go to hide and lick their wounds.

They were most afraid of unwarranted care, as they found it too confusing.

Xiang Yi blinked and changed her phrasing. "Where's the cafeteria?"

"Chua chua chua ——"

The sound of potatoes being peeled transmitted throughout the livestream through the audio equipment, comfortable and healing.

The girls fingers were slender and pale, but nimble as always. It took her a few seconds to peel a potato clean.

In the plastic basin next to her was a pile of peeled potatoes.

Li Jianyu: "Yimei… Are you cooking lunch for these kids?"

"There's not much else I can do." Xiang Yi responded quietly.

She continued to work, moving onto chopping meat after she finished peeling the potatoes. These ingredients had been delivered by people from Little Rabbit Farm.

In the back kitchen, there were only two pieces of frozen meat in the freezer. According to Rong Huai, they only got to eat a few pieces of minced meat on special occasions. The school's strange guidelines had one clause that stated too much meat would affect a child's IQ, so they recommended for all students to become vegetarian.

But the vegetables were not fresh at all; the potatoes had sprouted seeds, and all the greens were yellow and wilted.

It was impossible to imagine children eating these to grow up in a healthy manner.

Yin Jiangxue and Sheng Guang came in to help as well after hearing the news that Xiang Yi was going to cook for the kids.

Washing, cutting, and frying vegetables… What should have been boring and tedious tasks had become a wonderful chore with the girl's determined and calm demeanor.

The live comments regarding Xiang Yi's controversy still hung in the air:

——[What the hell, why are you cooking at a time like this? Take the kids home first]

——[What use is it going home? Get sent to this kind of "prestigious school" again by their parents to go through an X-Change?]

——[Actually I'm a little touched. Maybe people are just complicated creatures. Xiang Yi does have a bad side: she had hurt Dr. Xiang's arm, but she also has a good side, like helping the Sea7 boys and the kids from the boys high]

—— [Oh please, it's just one meal. Is she innocent now? Did you guys forget how disgusting that Xiang b*tch is?!!]

——[I'm new here. Don't know what you're arguing about, but I just want to say my mom got hungry from watching Xiang Yi cook with that metal spatula…]

The school cafeteria was full of big woks, tubs of rice, and large spatulas. It could basically be used as a shovel.

The shovel was heavy and the ingredients filled the big pot. It was even difficult for an a.d.u.l.t man to grasp the pot so well.

Some irresponsible chefs too lazy to cook would just boil water and sprinkle some spices in.

It was cooked thorough at best, but difficult to swallow.

But Xiang Yi took each pot of food seriously, as fine beads of sweat dripped from her pale forehead.

Without a word, she fried six woks of dishes.

By then, the rice was finished as well.

"You can start distributing food to the kids, I'm going to make a seaweed egg drop soup." Xiang Yi said to Rong Huai, who had been watching her for a long time.

The hardheaded tween felt his lips twitch. "You want me to hand out food to them?"

Xiang Yi didn't respond and just stared at him.

One second passed…

Two seconds…

Rong Huai couldn't take it anymore and turned his gaze away, saying dully,

"F*ck, don't look at me like that…"

"I get embarrassed, too."

chapters.title: Chapter 206 - Delicious
chapters.body:
Chapter 206 - Delicious

Meanwhile in the cafeteria, all the school's students gathered.

If this were any other school, hundreds of boys gathered together would have been a rowdy and noisy mess.

But here…

Not a single person made a sound, and the atmosphere was so quiet it seemed they could hear their breathing.

Nobody spoke with any expression, displaying the same numb and mechanical expression of a zombie.

It was unimaginably depressing.

Even more so, it was hard to imagine what kind of methods were used for these kids to 'obey' to such a degree.

Only…

The meal chute opened and the rich aroma of warm food spread through the atmosphere…

Instinctively, they began to hunger for the source of the smell.

Plate after plate of warm dishes were pushed out of the window.

Three vegetable dumplings…

Tomato fried egg…

Eggplant and brisket…

Vinegar stir-fried lettuce…

Cuc.u.mber stir-fried with pig intestines…

These were all very common canteen dishes, but they exuded a very rare and overwhelming delight.

Everyone's immediately thought was: hunger.

The devil-like chefs that worked the cafeteria on weekdays had disappeared and were replaced by Rong Huai and his buddies.

The boy uncomfortably banged on the end of a basin with a spoon, calling out: "Meal time, meal time! Grab your plates and line up!"

Nobody dared move, scared that this was a dream.

The group of kids eagerly stared at the food like stray dogs but did not dare to eat, hiding in the dark and dirty corners, afraid of approaching.

——[At this age, I can't watch these scenes; the tears won't stop]

——[Ahh, please eat! Everyone has to eat at least two bowls, okay!!]

——[How did you get kids to be more obedient than dogs? They've achieved their goal and succeeded, but their victory is the sorrow of these children]

Finally, someone couldn't help it and slowly took the first step approaching the cafeteria window.

Rong Huai unconsciously squeezed the spoon handle, feeling like a protagonist in a comic series who was tasked with the mission of saving the world.

"What do you want to eat?" He asked rudely, yet, he was generous with distributing the dishes. Every dish was scooped fully as if he was afraid these kids would not be full.

Once the first kid had stepped up, a second one lined up right after.

The line grew long.

Afterward, the first child to receive his meal took a bite of the dish, carefully placing it in his mouth, his eyes instantly widening..

Taste.

He was stunned for ten seconds before burying his face in his bowl and frantically began to eat as tears flowed down his cheeks.

More and more children began to frantically wolf down their food, all looking incredibly satiated as if they'd just eaten the most precious thing on earth.

Li Jianyu didn't understand at first. He had tasted the food, and despite Yi mei's incredible cooking, there was not much room for culinary advancement in just one large wok. This meal by comparison was nowhere near what she usually cooked.

Xiang Li could see his doubts as he nudged his silver-framed glasses and lowered his voice. "The best seasoning in the world is hunger."

"…" Immediately, Li Jianyu understood why Yi mei had insisted on cooking for these children.

Because…

They were really hungry.

He turned away and sniffed.

"F*ck… These sc.u.mbags!"

After serving the last bowl of soup, Rong Huai was so exhausted he could hardly raise his arms. However, when he saw Xiang Yi approaching, Rong Huai immediately straightened his back and resumed his cool demeanor.

The next second, a warm and fragrant steamed bun was stuffed into his mouth, accompanied by a gentle and soft voice.

"You've worked hard, go eat."

chapters.title: Chapter 207 - Why Are You So Good
chapters.body:
Chapter 207 - Why Are You So Good

Rong Huai was stunned for a moment, then said vaguely, "You think I'll eat just because you tell me to…"

Unconsciously, he began to chew.

Xiang Yi smirked. "Then spit it back out."

Rong Huai turned around. "No way! Hmph!"

Ah!

Amazing! Amazing! Amazing!!!

The pretty girl is too good at cooking!!!

Xiang Yi found a corner and sat down, rubbing at her sore wrists.

She was tired but didn't want to show it.

A tall figure loomed over, and Xiang Yi tilted her head to see Shi Sui.

He had been on the phone. "…Mhmm, once you deal with it send me an electronic version."

After hanging up, the man pulled out a travel-sized stick-on medication, about the size of two bandaids combined.

"Hands hurt?"

"Not bad."

As soon as Xiang Yi finished speaking, her small hands were pulled into the man's palm.

His hands were clean and warm to the touch.

She curled up her slender fingertips uncomfortably, attempting to retract her arm when Shi Sui gripped her wrist tightly.

"Don't move."

Subconsciously, Xiang Yi stopped moving.

"Zip——" Si Sui tore off the safety seal on the medicinal patch and carefully stuck it on the girl's slender wrists.

Xiang Yi's lips moved. "Actually… it doesn't hurt that much."

"Still acting tough?" A pair of pitch-black peach blossom eyes fell on her face. Shi Sui sighed helplessly. "Oh, you…"

Xiang Yi thought he was going to yell at her, but instead of reprimanding her, he rubbed her head twice.

"Why are you so nice…" he said.

Xiang Yi said quietly, "You've flatted my buns…"

Shi Sui laughed, dumbfounded. "What do you want me to do? Big brother doesn't know how to tie hair. Why not call your second brother to help?"

"Don't…"

How could she trouble her second brother with this kind of thing.

While talking, Xiang Li happened to pass by. Shi Sui beckoned him over to explain the situation.

Then, the knowledgeable medical doctor looked at his sister's collapsed pigtail buns and fell into a long silence.

"…" Xiang Li was quiet for a moment, then sincerely suggested: "Have you thought about cutting your hair short?"

Shi Sui sneered. "You don't know how to fix it?"

Xiang Li retorted, "You do?"

The two men who didn't know how to tie a bun began to childishly tease at each other.

Xiang Yi: "I'll do it myself…"

"Don't worry, I'll find a tutorial." Shi Sui calmly pulled out his phone and began looking for a guide.

Xiang Li raised his brows. Based on ability to learn, how could he lose to Shi Sui?

The two men became so engrossed with the hairstyle tutorial they couldn't help themselves.

A while later.

"Uhm…thank you, big sister, for cooking for us." A tall slender boy thanked her shyly while he stared at his toes.

Xiang Yi recognized him. "It's you…"

The boy who was on the rooftop just now.

The boy looked embarrassed. "Sorry, I…I must have embarrassed you. Sure enough, the teachers were right, I'm a useless piece of garbage…"

"That's not true." Xiang Yi said.

"You're not trash, and you're not useless."

"You probably don't know yet what your talents are, nor what your hobbies are."

"Maybe you will become an influential person in the future, maybe you will find interest in a certain field."

"You're still young, and the possibilities are endless. There are so many beautiful things in this world you haven't experienced yet."

"A lifetime is a long time. It might be painful now, but it's only a short period in what's to come."

"But life is irreversible and can't be retrieved once lost."

"So no matter how hard it gets, you must live on, and live on safely."

"You are an independent entity, so you yourself should be the meaning of your existence."

chapters.title: Chapter 208 - It'S Okay, You Can Cry
chapters.body:
Chapter 208 - It'S Okay, You Can Cry

The boy listened quietly as a hint of light glimmered on his lacquered pupils.

His family would always tell him, it's for his own good, they've done so much for him, yet he still let them down. Was he not sorry?

He had apologized numerous times to the point of hating himself.

He hated himself for not being a smart kid, hated himself for not being a lively child, and hated himself for not having a slick of emotional intelligence.

Coming to Mingxi Boys School was the beginning of his hell.

His body, personality, dignity, privacy… Every one of these aspects had been trampled underfoot.

Garbage, trash, coward, useless… These insulting words could be heard every minute.

But she said,

'You're important…'

'You must live on…'

'Your existence has a purpose…'

Unable to control himself, the boy s.u.c.k.e.d in a sob.

As if panicked by his outburst, he rubbed his eyes with his hands, trying to force the tears back, afraid of making the slightest sound.

But the more he wiped at his tears, the more fervently they flowed out.

To see a teenage boy crying like this would have been a funny scene, but at this moment, he looked inexplicably sad and pitiful.

Xiang Li was silent. He didn't quite understand how human beings felt since reason and logic always prevailed to him.

But Xiang Li knew his sister couldn't take it.

She looked distant, but this was only to shroud her soft heart.

Quietly, Xiang Li handed her a clean handkerchief.

Xiang Yi paused for a moment then received it.

There seems to be an invisible understanding between the siblings, that even without words, they understood each other's intentions.

Her brother was telling her to go console others.

Xiang Yi stood up and patted the boy on the back. Gently, she said: "Don't worry, you can cry."

The boy couldn't contain it anymore and began to bawl.

Emotions spilled out after being acc.u.mulated for a long time.

He covered his face with the handkerchief and cried wholeheartedly.

The camera captured this scene from a distance.

Although their mics were off and couldn't hear the sound, the body language was enough to transmit what was happening.

The most intuitive emotions were successfully expressed.

——[Oh my god, a brick fell in my eye]

——[ Sigh, just cry it out, it must suck so much for a child who can't even cry]

——[How should I say it, the Vase Fairy is kind of sh*tty, but she has a good side. Her gentle side is quite attractive]

Seeing Xiang Yi coax the boy, Rong Huai fiercely bit down on his sixth steamed bun.

So he knows how to cry, big whoop.

There was a sudden movement at the door, and there were sounds of crying shouting, and rapid footsteps.

They were the parents of the kids who had come after hearing the news.

An unsuspecting parent hugged their child as they cried in a huddled mess.

They only knew that the school was strict, so they were willing to pay higher tuition for their children. After their kids had been brainwashed, the children did not dare mention the mistreatment at home. Therefore, they had no idea the children suffered so much.

Some parents also anxiously asked the police at the scene: "Why are you shutting down such a good school? Where is our child going to go?! Are you guys mistaken? The principal and the teacher are doing this for the sake of the children!"

"Do you know this school uses severe physical punishment?"

"But our child's grades have improved, and he's become obedient, too. If you guys close the school, what if they go back to their former rebellious selves?"

chapters.title: Chapter 209 - Little Rabbit Farms 2.0
chapters.body:
Chapter 209 - Little Rabbit Farms 2.0

"…You can't just look for issues in your kids, parents have a responsibility in their children's education, too."

"We're so busy, how can we have time to educate our children? Please, I'm begging you, do not close the school…"

Listening to these conversations, a flash of disgust flashed in Rong Huai's eyes as he angrily ate another steamed bun.

Oh, how much he hated a.d.u.l.ts.

But as he habitually looked around, he didn't see his own parents. He paused his chewing for a moment, then returned to his cynical and cold demeanor.

It's okay.

He didn't need his parents anyways.

The young boy slowly ate his steamed buns, one after the another. As long as he ate enough, it would fill the disappointment in his heart.

Some parents saw Xiang Yi and began to accuse her without any explanation. "Why are you so nosy? Do you think you're a judge, bringing a whole production crew over here? Let me tell you, we don't need you! Now that the school is closing, how will you compensate us for our losses?"

Xiang Yi's gaze was calm as she waited for the woman to finish sputtering. Leisurely, she responded: "I forgot to tell you, our show is a livestream."

"Ah——!!" The scream was immediately followed by a slew of curse words. "Why didn't you say so?!"

The photographer Lao Zhang aided in the ruse as he deliberately jutted the camera towards them, scaring the screaming party as they immediately covered their faces with their jacket.

Lao Zhang was speechless for a while. "So you have shame? I thought you weren't human!"

Shi Sui answered the phone after being disrupted. Slowly, he adjusted his cuffs and asked Xiang Yi in a lazy tone: "Do you need more land to farm?"

"Hm?"

"It turns out I have some disposable income, so I've bought this land the school currently resides on." Shi Sui then glanced at the parents. "The school is mine now. How about excavating it into a vegetable field? Or a pigsty? Last time, the black pork meat was very delicious…"

Xiang Yi: "…"

Buy the land…for farming? Raising pigs?

She pondered it over for a few seconds, actually feeling quite happy with the idea.

"Why not turn this into Little Rabbit Farm 2.0?" Xiang Li said gently. "I'll contact the research center and see if we can use this area as a laboratory base for cultivating Chinese Medicine Research."

With the endors.e.m.e.nt of the research institute, the school was done for.

Xiang Yi's eyes lit up.

How amazing!

The person covering their eyes with their jacket was dumbfounded.

Buying land, research institute and scientific endors.e.m.e.nt, where were these people from?!

All people suspected were taken away for investigation.

The students gathered at the school gate as they watched the gates close bit by bit.

But several peoples' expressions were still extremely sour, worried that these gates would be reopened one day.

Suddenly.

A rumbling noise sounded from the distance. Someone inexplicably shouted "f*ck" as chaos ensued.

Construction vehicles, demolition machines, excavators… countless vehicles pulled up, honking their horns.

"Beep—— Beep——"

In the operator's seat, dressed in a bespoke suit and leather shoes, Si Chuanbai's face was expressionless as if his heart was like the calm surface of a lake.

When Shi Sui noticed him, the man crossed his arm and began to chuckle coldly.

Si Chuanbai: "…"

As a perfectly dignified master class manager, he would accept being suddenly asked to purchase land, but who knew he also had to work part time as an excavator operator.

His life was so hard, so so hard.

Sheng Guang had also driven to the school with his wife Yin Jiangxue. The two of them were holding a newly made plaque in their hands.

The elegant and unstrained script read:

Little Rabbit Farm 2.0!

chapters.title: Chapter 210 - Source Of Light
chapters.body:
Chapter 210 - Source Of Light

The livestream:

——[Ahh this is too crazy!]

——[Holy sh*t, are they going to demolish the whole school?!]

——[I've held it in for so long, but I still ended up crying]

——[I just want to say Yi mei is such a badass! Shi Yingdi is such a badass! "Two Under One Roof" is so badass!]

In the live comments, there was no more hate speech, only solidarity.

When the first demo vehicle knocked down the school wall, the dusty scene suddenly erupted with a huge noise.

Cheers and roars of applause…turned into a blood-boiling melody.

Rong Huai stuck one hand in his pocket as his face slowly formed into a smile.

"You guys went to steal things on purpose, didn't you." The girl's soft and sweet voice sounded above the clamor. Rong Huai's body stiffened as he uncomfortably fiddled with his ear.

"I'm not that kind of nosy person." The boy continued to deny it.

Xiang Yi looked at him and spoke a few key words. "Stealing, streaming, making a big splash, calling the police, exposing the school…This was your plan, right?"

Rong Huai's expression was stiff.

How did she so casually guess the brilliant idea that the few of them had planned for such a long time?

The boy kicked a nearby rock and awkwardly responded, "Are you trying to say our plan is naive… I mean, we…we want to become m.a.t.u.r.e, too…"

But they didn't know what to do.

After hearing that there was a livestream happening in the manor next door, even if they had never seen it before nor fully understood the impact "Two Under One Roof" would have, they still pursued their plans with reckless passion. Without hesitation, they had broken in, scared to cause too much damage. It had taken them a long time of mental preparation to even steal those eggs…

After a few seconds, Rong Huai did not respond, but he couldn't help secretly glancing at Xiang Yi.

The girl met his gaze, her beautiful eyes curving into little half moons.

"You guys did good."

Thump thump thump…

She's praising me…

His heartbeat sped up as it pounded densely like a drum.

If there was a fawn in his heart, it would probably be dizzy from bumping around and had a concussion.

Rong Huai moved his feet and approached her, arching his thin back and coughing twice.

"Uhm… come on, do it."

"Huh?"

Rong Huai looked at her blank expression and irritably said, "Do that just now!" His voice turned quiet as he capriciously said with a hint of grievance. "You have already patted that guy on the back, but you haven't done it to me…"

The next second, the girl stood on her tiptoes and with her soft pale hands, imitated Shi Sui's coaxing method and rubbed his head a few times.

Rong Huai froze, his whole body dumbfounded.

She…She patted my head…

His heart exploded into fireworks, but his expression was a proud one. "You're so short, you have to stand on your toes to pat my head."

Xiang Yi curled her lips. "Your hair is so prickly… Like a hedgehog…"

The young boy felt his hair go on edge. "You don't know sh*t! My style is hedgehog hair!"

"…" Okay then.

On the plaque, there was a thin wooden board nailed onto it.

Yin Jiangxue: "Little Xiang Yi, this is your farm, so you should put this up."

Everyone looked toward Xiang Yi.

Not far away, the machines roared as buildings collapsed and dust raged like the setting of a doomsday movie.

The shadows from the trees whirled as the young girl stood on the edge of shadow and light with a calm and gentle gaze.

At that moment, a phrase suddenly popped into Rong Huai's mind.

——Everything in the universe is damaged, but she is the source of the light.

chapters.title: Chapter 211 - Don'T Forget To Eat
chapters.body:
Chapter 211 - Don'T Forget To Eat

Xiang Yi blinked and graciously responded: "Okay."

She received the simple plaque from Yin Jiangxue.

The sentiment dissipated for a short while as haters populated the live stream once again.

——[What the heck, what's the point of this]

——[Shouldn't you be punishing the bad guys and thinking about what to do with these students?]

——[This is not the time to be paying attention to rituals! Sheng Guang is just a poor artist. Instead of writing this, he should have just donated some money to the kids]

Rong Huai thought about helping Xiang Yi search for a shovel to dig a hole, but before he could get to it, the girl picked a patch of soft soil and jammed the plaque and its post into the ground!

Jammed it in!

Rong Huai: ???

The livestream viewers: ???

Although it was muddy, but still… Sister, how much strength do you have?!

"From now on, this is no longer Mingxi Boys High School." Xiang Yi turned to the boys with a smile. "It is Little Rabbit Farm 2.0."

In the crowd, some cried bitterly while others cheered.

Regardless if they were happy or sad, all the kids finally had expressions on their faces.

In the stream, the fans poked back.

——[Xiang Yi and Shi Yingdi aren't police officers, how do you suppose they can punish them?]

——[What else do you know aside from clacking on a keyboard? Did you help them make food? Did you excavate the school? You didn't do sh*t, so shut up!]

——[Ritual should never be used as a derogatory term. It is the most direct way for a person to express their internal thoughts. For these children, what matters most is not that the school closes formally, but that they reconstruct their psychological beliefs and values. From a certain point of view, Xiang Yi jamming the Little Rabbit Farm sign into the ground signals the end of these children's past suffering! It basically revived a group of people who didn't have any d.e.s.i.r.e to live! This is a very positive act of reinforcement!]

The cleanup was left to the professionals.

Under everyone's watchful eye, Xiang Yi calmly…stepped into the tractor.

Shi Sui and Xiang Li, basically two people with the figures of male models, once again sandwiched her between them.

The girl protested in a small voice. "You can sit in the back too…"

Shi Sui calmly retorted, "Your tractor doesn't even have a rear view mirror, I'll help you keep an eye out."

Xiang Li: "Useless courtesy."

Seeing that the two were about to start another argument, Xiang Yi could only act as mediator. "Okay, okay, let's go."

"Hey——" Rong Huai loudly announced. "You…You're leaving just like that? You're not gonna say something?"

The staff on site also had similar expectations, thinking Yi mei would say something to encourage the healing process in everyone.

The girl pursed her lips and left them with a plain sentence.

"No matter what you run into, remember to eat."

Afterward, she started the tractor and left.

Everyone: …???

That's it?

You're done?

Someone yelled out: "Big Sister's words are very meaningful. Only when your stomach is satisfied do you have the energy and strength to use your brain, and only then will your soul have the power to resist! This way, no matter what difficulties we encounter, we will be able to overcome them!"

The live comments: [Is that you, language arts class representative?!]

This interpretation undoubtedly lifted the spirits of the children.

The foggy haze that was hanging over everyone's heads was beginning to dissipate little by little.

Back at the manor.

Xiang Yi was worried about Xiao Naofu and decided to make it some cat food when she suddenly heard Xiang Li speak: "Bunbun, my hand hurts."

Hearing this, Shi Sui raised his slender eyebrows.

chapters.title: Chapter 212 - 212 Acting Contest 1
chapters.body:
Chapter 212 - 212 Acting Contest 1

Hearing that her brother's wrist hurt, Xiang Yi instantly became anxious. "How does it hurt? Let me see!"

She grabbed Xiang Li's hand and carefully examined it.

The comments reacted accordingly.

——[Ahh ah b*tch Xiang, let go of my doctor gege's hand!]

——[You are the main cause of Doctor Xiang's hand injury, what are you pretending for?]

——[Has Xiang Li gege been poisoned by her? Why is he calling her Bunbun so intimately? Is she worthy???]

…

The man's joints were distinctive and pale like jade. Because of an annual lack of sunlight, his skin was almost transparent.

There was no external injury to his hand as the girl pressed several acupuncture points in turn, asking how he felt.

Xiang Li said nonchalantly, "It's not that it hurts, I'm probably just tired."

"Then you take a seat, I'll go bring you some herbal medicine and give you a massage after."

Swiftly, she brought the medicine over.

Xiang Li scooped the brew with his right hand, but the spoon fell with a 'clang' against the rim of the bowl as his hand lost control.

Xiang Yi's heart throbbed as she worriedly bit her lip.

My brother's hand…had been damaged this severely?!

"I…I'll feed you?" She asked carefully.

Xiang Li's eyelids drooped as the mole under his eye loomed. "It's okay, I'll just use my left hand."

It was pitiful.

Shi Sui: "…"

Okay, your acting is respectable.

Xiang Yi watched with puppy eyes as her brother drank the medicine. She wiped his hands with a warm towel and continued to gently and meticulously massage his wrist.

Her technique was professional, and her strength well moderated. Xiang Li was enjoying it so much his eyes squinted comfortably.

Xiang Yi massaged his wrist for a solid ten minutes.

"Better?" she asked.

"It's alright." Xiang Li's lips curved into a bitter grimace. "I'm used to it."

Xiang Yi felt even worse. She vowed in her heart that she would heal her brother's hand no matter what!

Suddenly, Xiao Naofu, deprived of attention, came over to protest. It hopped into Xiang Yi's arms and smacked her shoulder with its creamy white paws as if accusing the sc.u.mmy girl:

"Meow meow meow!" It attempted in a pitchy roar.

"Just wait a minute, I'll go make you lunch right now," Xiang Yi said apologetically.

Xiao Naofu rubbed its head against her collarbone as if it'd been wronged. "Meow~meowu~"

Shi Sui: "…"

Wonderful, even this stupid cat is trying to join in on this.

Xiang Yi headed for the kitchen.

Xiang Li carefully picked up Xiao Naofu and put it on his knees. The small cat was curled into a ball and about the same size as his palm.

Toward the second brother, whom Xiao Naofu was not familiar with, the cat eagerly rolled over to reveal its belly, fl.i.c.k.i.n.g its tail twice as it waited for the gentle and elegant man to service him with pets.

It never thought——

A dark light flashed across the man's silver-rimmed glasses for a second. "Male? A little bit older and you will need to be neutered."

Xiao Naofu: ???

Shi Sui rarely reached an agreement, but this time their views aligned. "You should choose a good and auspicious day."

Xiao Naofu was terrified as it curled its tail; watery eyes filled with tears.

Q_Q These two men are so scary…

Xiang Li glanced at the luxurious three-storey cat condo in the distance and frowned. "Most cats can just sleep in a shoebox."

Shi Sui: "A delivery box works, too."

The stream viewers were dying of laughter.

——[These two men won't even let a cat off the hook!]

——[Xiao Naofu in Xiang Yi's presence: Fierce and majestic. Xiao Naofu when Xiang Yi is absent: Pitiful and cowardly]

——[Shi Yingdi x Dr. X x Little Kitty, they're basically a family of three! I ship this CP, I ship it!]

chapters.title: Chapter 213 - 213 Acting Contest 2
chapters.body:
Chapter 213 - 213 Acting Contest 2

…

Xiang Yi came out of the kitchen at this moment with a bowl of cat food.

Xiao Naofu saw its savior, and it pranced over, cowardly hiding beside the girl's slender ankles.

"What's wrong, Xiao Naofu?" Xiang Yi bent over, squeezing its ears.

Xiao Naofu butted her palm, scared to make a sound.

"Here are some pats, time to eat." Xiang Yi placed the bowl in front of the cream-colored cat and gently coaxed it.

Xiao Naofu turned its grief into appetite and buried its face in the chicken and pumpkin rice.

Xiang Yi looked toward the two men on the sofa and asked with uncertainty, "You two, did you bully Xiao Naofu?"

Although she knew that her brother and Shi Sui were not that naïve, Xiao Naofu looked way too pitiful to ignore…

Shi Sui looked wronged. "Huh? Do you think I am the kind of person who would neglect a cat?"

The man's question stunned Xiang Yi for a moment.

Xiao Naofu raised its head from the bowl, glaring with its large pupils.

You motherf*cker!

Can you at least try to act like a human!!

The girl hesitated for a long time before slowly shaking her head.

Xiang Li's expression did not waver as he calmly looked at his little sister.

Xiang Yi became conflicted once again.

She scratched Xiao Naofu's chin and whispered about her brother, "He wouldn't do such boring things…You have to behave in the future."

Xiao Naofu's expression was one of shock.

Does this Huhu mean so little to you?

These two sc.u.mbag men are playing me!!!

Xiao Naofu angrily turned its bum toward Xiang Yi and continued to eat.

This season's acting contest… Shi Yingdi's Mr. Day and Mr. Night get one point each!

But soon, the two men were incapable of being happy.

Xiang Yi cleaned up after the cat finished eating and began to brush its fur, feed it vitamins, give it its ear drops, brush its teeth…

This was it!

Menacing glares from the two men were almost sharp enough to burn a hole through Xiao Naofu…

Xiang Yi then played around with Xiao Naofu for a bit, teasing it with a bouncing ball. She kissed its forehead before placing it on the cat bed to settle down.

The cat faced her with its back, still upset.

Xiang Yi was shaking the cat hammock when she heard her second brother call.

"Bunbun, my hand hurts again."

Eh?

This frequently?

"I'll check your pulse?"

"Okay."

Since this concerned her brother's hand, the girl was extra focused. She removed her hand from his vein after a long couple of minutes.

It was more serious than she'd expected.

If it had been the Xiang Yi before her transmigration, there was nothing she would have been able to do.

But now, she was certain she could cure him!

"Do you want to try acupuncture? It's a safe estimate to say you only need a few sessions before you start seeing results."

The viewers on stream began to comment again.

——[You? Do you want to turn the doctor into a paraplegic!]

——[You bringer of hate, leave the doctor gege alone! If you touch him, you're going to defile a pure flower!]

——[You think it will succeed just because you say so? What if you fail? Who's going to take responsibility?]

The comments were in a heated argument when they heard the girl say in a serious tone: "I can write down the prescription, and you can show it to a doctor. If it works, then you can let someone else administer the treatment."

It wasn't that she didn't have the skills to do it herself; she was afraid Xiang Li didn't trust her.

The audience: ???

Xiang Li smiled politely, revealing the red mole at the edge of his eye. He reached out, the light blue vessels on his pale wrist visible, and warmly said:

"You should do it."

"Our Bunbun is trustworthy."

chapters.title: Chapter 214 - 214 Can You Coax Me
chapters.body:
Chapter 214 - 214 Can You Coax Me

Xiang Yi thought she was imagining things when she heard this.

The girl raised her little face, unable to respond.

"Bunbun is already twenty, but you're still so dumb…" Xiang Li's voice sounded helplessly indulgent. "Why are you frozen there…are you not going to take a chance to bully me?"

When they were little, Xiang Li had trolled everyone in his vicinity from his little brother and sister to his uncle, with the exception of big brother…

Xiang Yi finally reacted.

She took a deep breath and solemnly said, "I will definitely do my best."

The girl then stood up to retrieve her acupuncture tools as the comment section of the stream went into a frenzy once again.

——[He was just being courteous, and you took it for real? Do you not know where your real medical skills f*cking lie?]

——[I'm so lost. Do these men only think with the bottom half of their body? It's like they've lost their souls when they see the Vase Fairy]

——[Ahh I'm so angry, the PhD gege is a national treasure in scientific research, does he not have a bodyguard or something to take down this Xiang B*tch? If you let her continue like this, what if PhD gege's hand is damaged for good?!]

…

In reality, there was a group of special forces hiding in the shadows sent to protect Xiang Li.

Xiang Li held his own opinions, however, and nobody dared disobey him. They could only report his activities to their superiors.

Soon, Xiang Li received a call.

His expression remained calm. "Hm…I trust her."

"She would never hurt me."

"I will bear all the consequences alone, so don't hold her responsible."

——[PhD gege, why is your memory so bad? She was the one who had hurt you, remember??]

——[Ah, this is not in my area of knowledge. What's going on? Does the show have some hidden motives?]

——[Hidden motives, my ass; the video is real. The person rock climbing was Xiang Yi, no doubt! Can her fans stop trying to validate her? It's embarrassing!]

——[I'm nobody's fan, but I should be considerably older than most of the people on this stream. To be fair, even if Xiang Yi had harmed Xiang Li, none of us has the right to forgive her on his behalf. As long as he doesn't mind, nobody has the right to judge him.]

…

The viewers in the stream quarreled endlessly.

Meanwhile, Xiang Yi brought a medical kit over and slowly disinfected the acupuncture needles. Her expression was unprecedentedly serious.

Those who were focused always exuded an attractive aura.

Many people in front of the screen were attracted to this as the hate speech lessened for a moment.

"I'm a bit scared." Xiang Li innocently looked at his sister. "Can you coddle me?"

Shi Sui: "…"

How could someone who dared to dissect a rabbit at the age of seven be scared of this?

Bullsh*t. 🙂

Xiang Yi bit her lips. She didn't really know how to coax people aside from cooking and handing out candy.

But now, she couldn't just leave to cook, and her second brother didn't like sweets…

Anxiously, she blurted out: "Big brother, can you be scared later?"

The staff on-site who were plastered in a cold sweat: "…"

Yi mei, it's usually go eat later, never be scared later!

Unexpectedly, Xiang Li's eyebrows formed into a smile and gently obeyed.

"Okay, I'll try to wait."

The viewers: This f*cking works??

Xiang Yi's expression was tense. "Okay, then I'm going to begin."

Whoosh ——

The first needle fell precisely on the acupuncture point…

chapters.title: Chapter 215 - Heart Is Vile
chapters.body:
Chapter 215 - Heart Is Vile

Afterward, the second, the third needle…

Depending on different acupuncture points, the needles' depth may vary in shallowness, speed, twist, or flick…

The focused girl deliberately slowed her actions. In the eyes of ordinary people, her needle speed was lightning fast!

Viewers in the stream began to take on the role of armchair physicians once again:

——[Nobody can find acupuncture points that fast! At least feel out the point first!]

——[Wow, has the Xiang woman been watching too much TV, or has she just been watching on 2.0 speed? Nobody does acupuncture this fast…]

——[I am a TCM student. What kind of acupuncture points are these? Plus, her actions pursue speed over results. Obviously, she's just trying to show off! Either way, I have never seen a TCM doctor practice like this!]

…

They waited for the puncture points to take effect.

Xiang Yi finally relaxed her muscles as Shi Sui handed her a napkin at the right time. She wiped her forehead, soaking the tissue with sweat.

She asked her brother, "How do you feel?"

Xiang Li was silent for a few moments. "Hm… The places you've punctured feel warm, almost hot from within. Aside from that, there's nothing strange."

After his injury, he had visited all the famous doctors around the world and even invited several domestic TCM masters to treat him.

But at most, the puncture points only felt a little warm but never burning hot as it did now.

"It's normal to feel the heat." Xiang Yi let out a sigh. "If you're not scared of needles, then we'll leave it in for ten minutes. Once it comes out, it should recover some of your function."

Xiang Li's eyes lit up. "How sure are you?!"

He did not regret saving his sister. Even though the mind wasn't hers, it was still his sister's body!

But he'd be lying to say he had no regrets.

Someone as proud as Xiang Li had become a cripple in the field he was best at.

Often, he felt frustrated and powerless.

Yet now, his sister was saying his hand could regain feeling again!

Even if it was just a little, he was satisfied!

Xiang Yi looked at him with an inexplicable expression. "Since I was the one who treated you, of course I'm 100% sure."

She thought her big brother was disappointed she couldn't completely heal him and gently explained, "Don't worry, even if it's a little at a time, I promise, with the right prescription and cupping methods, you will be cured within five treatments! Then your hands will heal completely!"

Xiang Li stared at her for a moment, then exhaled. "Okay, I will cooperate with our Bunbun."

The seemingly aloof young doctor felt his heart roll with turbulent waves.

His hand can be healed!

Not maybe, can be!!

Perhaps if it were someone else, he would still question their assurance.

But he unconditionally believed everything his little sister said!

Little did they know, Xiang Yi's remarks had aroused the dissatisfaction of the viewers.

The blogger who was first offended on behalf of Xiang Li once again published a long text:

[Noob and Bald PhD]: I thought @XiangYi was just young and ignorant, but I didn't think you were a poisonous woman! Do you know how depressed Dr. X has been this year? He had to hand off his most prized research to someone else, not because he didn't want to continue, but because he couldn't experiment anymore! That was a research project that could have won a Nobel Prize! And he handed it off just like that!

Your brain is so stupid and selfish, with pompous medical skills full of ostentatious exaggeration. Now you even dare say Dr. X can 'definitely' be cured?! Do you think the IQ of a genius is this low? Where do you place all the famous doctors whose attempts have failed over the last few years? Was it not enough for you to harm Dr. X's body that you have to destroy his will?!

This is despicable!! Your heart is vile!!!

chapters.title: Chapter 216 - Young Prodigy
chapters.body:
Chapter 216 - Young Prodigy

This particular Weibo post caused mass controversy.

——[Well spoken, a doctorate nonetheless!]

——[Being cultured is so different, I only know how to call people a dumbass…]

——[My god, so the Vase Fairy is cheating our Dr. Xiang? How could she do that!]

——[I want to cry again. How can the PhD gege trust her so much? If he knew he was being lied to, he would be so sad…]

…

Netizens who wanted to follow the drama began forwarding and liking one comment after another, sending the search term to trend on Weibo.

Several people continuously harassed the bigwigs of the scientific research community, wanting them to come forward and make a statement.

However, the strange thing was none of these experts said a word.

Some haters decided to find another way and began tagging the Chinese Traditional Medicine Society, egging them on: [Shouldn't this kind of blind practice result in having their TCM license revoked? Her skills are not good, and she uses them solely for the purpose of showing off]

"Whoosh."

A notice popped up on the live comments as the haters clicked the link to realize the TCM association had responded to their request:

[Hello! Who is this prodigy doctor? Where can we find her?? I will provide a hefty reward!!]

The haters: ???

The hell? Official Site-san, did you get hacked?

The man hesitated, then typed: [Thanks, but no thanks. She's called the Vase Fairy and just a permanent guest on a popular variety show]

After sending that out, he felt something was off. After reading over it several times, he hurriedly followed up with another comment:

[Eh, sorry, I 'm too used to calling her that. I almost believed her last name is Vase. Her name is Xiang Yi, and this is her Weibo @XiangYi]

…

A few seconds later.

Several TCM masters in China began receiving calls from the chairman of the TCM association:

Emergency virtual meeting!

Ten minutes later, dozens of masters from the practice gathered in the virtual conference room of a teamspeak software.

Each individual could be considered a prodigy-level physician.

The host of the meeting was the president himself. He was in his twilight years with deep wrinkles on his face, but with a strong spirit and raven black hair.

Several masters have secretly discussed behind the chairman's back regarding his hair, and if he was wearing a wig or using hair dye…

"The purpose of the meeting is to show everybody this video."

The president made a gesture as the young boy next to him played a video. His exciting voice could be heard. "Grandpa, I've confirmed it, this clip is real! And this prodigy physician is only twenty years old!"

Can a twenty-year-old these days be called a prodigy physician?

Several masters began to murmur discreetly. When they were twenty years old, they were still being disciplined by their masters…

At this moment, the video began to play on everyone's screen.

With one casual glance, the audience was instantly mesmerized!

The girl in the video seemed calm and indifferent, yet her needle placements seemed to be guided by god's hand himself. The acupuncture points, the placement, the movement, to the duration, to the removal…every step under her hands seemed exaggerated and sped up to a regular person, but in the eyes of these Xinglin masters…

She was a genius!!!

…

Ten minutes later, after the video had ended, several people realized they were holding their breaths unconsciously.

"Who is this young prodigy? Where does she live?" Someone burst out.

"Hey, Old Xu, are you trying to poach someone from under my nose?"

"Do you think the young prodigy will want to work at your dumb little practice? Why not come be my disciple, my practice is large!"

"You guys are all so ugly, stop dreaming! I'm going to say this now—where does the young prodigy live! I'm going to approach her doorstep and accept her as my disciple!"

chapters.title: Chapter 217 - Helpful Citizen
chapters.body:
Chapter 217 - Helpful Citizen

As everyone was in a heated discussion.

Jing Lao, labeled the "poisonous doctor" sneered. "Disciple? You want to take them, but does she want to accept? Her acupuncture technique is enough to become a master to several of you!"

The virtual meeting fell into a strange silence.

Jing Lao specialized in fighting poison with poison when it came to medicine, but the title 'poisonous doctor' had formed because of his vile tongue.

He had a strange personality and a toxic mouth and has even offended several powerful patients.

The room was silent for a moment.

Yun Lao, who specialized in nutritional therapy began to speak. "The girl's acupuncture skills are superb indeed, but I can teach her in the area of nutritional therapy."

Yun Lao had a good rapport, and soon many people chided in after him.

"Yes, yes, yes, I'm very good at massages! I have a lot to offer, more than enough to teach her!"

"When it comes to moxibustion, who dare call themselves number one in this room if I say I am second?"

"The girl is too slow at checking the pulse. I am afraid she has insufficient training in pulse reading, and this old man can teach her how to see and hear it."

"…"

Soon, the conference room suddenly became a stage for showing off.

The elders of the TCM association were glowing with pride, closing their eyes to rest their minds.

Nowadays, TCM has been strongly overshadowed by Western medicine, its impact fading. There weren't as many people willing to learn TCM, the numbers inferior to Western medicine.

After all this time, it was indeed difficult to find a good seed to cultivate.

Medicine, like any discipline, required geniuses.

And the girl in this video had displayed a master level of acupuncture and moxibustion at the age of twenty! There was no doubt she was a genius!

If they could properly cultivate her growth and gather everyone's strengths, they could revitalize the TCM world. That would be incredible!

Thinking of this, the president coughed a few times as everyone fell silent immediately.

"My grandson says she's been filming a show. I want to appoint some people to participate in her show and see if the girl is interested in apprenticeship."

Everyone responded enthusiastically, expressing their d.e.s.i.r.e to be chosen.

…

Meanwhile, at the manor.

Xiang Yi packed up her needles and organized her tools.

Xiang Li flexed his right hand, a flash of shock displayed in his eyes.

Really…he could feel it!

Before, his hand could only perform some simple movements, such as picking up a book. But now, he could write and draw!

Xiang Li couldn't help but show a moved expression as he whispered, "Our Bunbun is really amazing."

The viewers: ???

It really worked??

Xiang Yi wasn't playing around?!

Suddenly, there was a motion at the door as a staff member entered and whispered in Li Jianyu's ear.

A few seconds later, Li Jianyu became fl.u.s.tered. "Uhm… Yi mei, can you come to the door? There are police here."

The haters were back. [The police are here to take her away! Hahaha what a nice sight to see!]

The next second ——

Li Jianyu's voice echoed through the stream.

"He says they're here to give you a pennant."

The anti-fans: ???

…

Xiang Yi went to the entrance of the manor, still in a daze.

Outside the iron gates were two parked blue police cars.

The policemen in uniform saluted her when they saw Xiang Yi approach, then with a grin, handed over a big red banner.

In bold text, it read:

[ Angel of Light, Helpful Citizen! ]

"Thank you, Miss Xiang Yi, for making great contributions to our investigation of the case of Mingxi Boys High School."

Xiang Yi: "…"

Aside from the little red flag she had received in elementary school, she had never gotten any other banners.

"Thank you, it's my duty."

The netizens watching the stream were dumbfounded.

They've only seen civilians give pennants to the police, but the opposite can happen too?!

This was so badass!!!

chapters.title: Chapter 218 - Cp Specialist
chapters.body:
Chapter 218 - Cp Specialist

…

The stream was in chaos.

——[Official recognition]

——[wtf? This isn't scripted, right??]

——[She's only a regular, warm-hearted citizen…?]

——[Everything else aside, let's discuss this rationally. How badass is it for a female artist in the entertainment industry to personally receive a pennant from the police?]

At this moment, the anti-fans stared blankly at their keyboards, unable to speak.

How can we hate on that?

How the f*ck can we hate on that?!

This Xiang Yi is poisonous!

As a female celebrity, there were always red carpet mishaps, or airport and street shots, or the occasional romance scandal.

Planting flowers, cooking, catching pigs, playing the Sanxian, practicing TCM… She could randomly drive a tractor to send kids to school, and save all the children from said school!

Thinking about it, she was even…a little charismatic?

With this communal pennant, and her effective acupuncture on Xiang Li, the comment section became much friendlier.

——[QQ I was scared to speak, but I can't help it anymore. Yi mei knows she did something wrong and tried to improve. She's still a good kid!]

——[Yiyi did screw up indeed, and it has caused everyone a lot of strife, we admit. But we hope everyone can see the good parts of her personality ❤]

——[I am really confused now, I feel like the Vase Fairy now is so different from before! It's like she's two different people…]

——[You're too on point! I used to like watching videos in the occult section of the stream site that cut scenes of the Vase Fairy on different variety shows. Since she's gone on a lot of them, there is a lot of footage. She's basically an entertainment black hole though. It's fine if you're just awkward, but she likes to cry, likes to show off, act fake… But now, she's a completely different person! I even began to doubt if she had a twin]

…

Xiang Yi returned to the living room with her flag.

Shi Sui chuckled. "Want me to hang it for you?"

Xiang Yi was embarrassed. "No, no, let's put it away. I'm going to go check on Xiao Naofu, it's still ignoring me."

Next to the French windows, Xiang Li looked gentle and polite as his pale fingers flipped through the pages of a book. Lightly, he said, "Male creatures can't be spoiled. If you spoil them, they will be spoiled rotten."

The words "male creature" had another meaning.

Shi Sui raised his eyebrows and looked over.

Xiang Li looked back.

Their gazes met, and an invisible electrical current crackled and exploded as they confronted each other.

The girl suddenly exclaimed. "Xiao Naofu is a boy?!"

Shi Sui: "…"

Xiang Li: "…"

The two men looked toward Xiang Yi as the girl was curiously reaching to lift the cat's fluffy tail…

"!"

Suddenly, Shi Sui grabbed her wrist. She could feel the warmth of his fingertips. "It's not good to take advantage of Xiao Naofu like this, right?"

Xiang Yi explained in a small voice: "No, I'm just curious, I want to see… Sigh…." Her pale ears gradually turned red.

She had never raised a cat before and had never studied Xiao Naofu's biological composition. Plus, when she was transmigrating, Xiao Naofu was just her system, which had a mechanical sound that was difficult to distinguish gender.

She'd always been sure… Xiao Naofu was a girl…

Shi Sui smiled as his peach blossom eyes squinted lazily. "So straightforward? The cat will be shy."

In the live broadcast room:

——[???? Am I the only one with a dirty mind?]

——[Shi Yingdi is so flirty, even I'm blushing]

——[I'm screaming louder than a chicken! Nobody cover my mouth!!]

——[Sorry, mom; after growing up, I couldn't become a scientist, but I can become a CP specialist!]

——[Sisters, Untimely CP —— It's happening!!]

chapters.title: Chapter 219 - Do You Think The Bunbun'S Second Brother Is Dead?
chapters.body:
Chapter 219 - Do You Think The Bunbun'S Second Brother Is Dead?

The girl murmured hesitantly. "But, it…it doesn't have that…"

"What?" Shi Sui responded teasingly. It was unclear whether he really didn't understand, or if he was just deliberately teasing her.

"You know, that…"

Laughter rang in her ears. The two of them were so close Xiang Yi could almost hear the vibration in his c.h.e.s.t.

Their wrists brushed against each other, entwined by an invisible string.

A slight itch.

The irritability of entanglement.

Shi Sui leaned over and turned off their microphones. He whispered a few words in her ear and saw the results as he wished…

The crimson hue of the bunny's ears was seemingly obvious.

Xiang Yi: …!!

Is that so!!

Xiao Naofu squinted at Xiang Yi in disgust from its hammock as it licked its paw.

It's fine if she can't tell my gender, but she also can't tell how evil the two men in the room were!

Especially Shi Sui, that stinky man. He's worse than a dog!

A few seconds later.

Shi Sui suppressed a chuckle as he said in a low voice, "You get it?"

Xiang Yi mumbled a "mhmm".

Not far away, Xiang Li loudly flipped his book pages and coldly said, "Is our Bunbun's hand something you can just hold if you want to?"

Ah, so the hunter becomes the hunted.

Did Shi Sui think Bunbun's second brother was dead? 🙂

It was then Xiang Yi realized, Shi Sui had been holding her wrist…

She retracted her arm as if she'd just been burned, feeling self-conscious. "I…I'm going to go make a cake. Mentor Yin should be back soon."

The girl hurriedly retreated into the kitchen.

Shi Sui looked toward Xiang Li, uttering a few silent phrases through his thin lips.

He turned his back to the camera so the audience couldn't see him, but Xiang Li understood what he was saying with ease ——

[ Don't be angry, second uncle-in-law ]

In Nancheng, men would only call their wife's elder brother 'uncle-in-law'.

Xiang Li ground his teeth silently. I treated you like a brother, but you're trying to date my sister?

You're dreaming!

…

Stretching into the pale mountains a fair distance away from the manor, Sheng Guang had been walking with his wife. He took off his jacket and placed it over her. "Honey, take a break."

Ever since returning from Mingxi Boys school, Yin Jiangxue had been in a worrisome state.

She gathered her husband's jacket around her and sighed. "In this lifetime, some things will just not go your way. Some people don't get what they ask for, while others have to suffer it. I really… feel sorrow on behalf of those children."

Yin Jiangxue loved children and had been trying very hard over the years.

First, she had asked physicians all over the city and even prayed to the Buddha.

Still, she wasn't able to get pregnant.

If she had a child, she would treasure them wholeheartedly.

But how could the parents of the boys…Yin Jiangxue closed her eyes, unable to imagine it anymore.

Gently, Sheng Guang said: "A child is like fate. You can't force it, so we should just let things happen naturally."

Yin Jiangxue's red lips quivered. Perhaps the affairs at Mingxi Boys school had shocked her too much, for she began to speak her mind. "Actually…I've asked a master physician to prescribe me a type of medicine that could increase my chance of pregnancy, but it's quite damaging to the body. I don't think I'll be able to act anymore, but I'd like to try it…"

Sheng Guang objected deeply. "No! Your health of utmost priority right now!"

The stream viewers immediately began to curse.

——[Yin Jiangxue, is your brain broken?]

——[What? I don't understand this kind of woman, why does she HAVE to have a child?]

——[Barf…The man is okay, but this girl can't be saved! The Qing Dynasty is gone, but you're still thinking of continuing the bloodline?]

chapters.title: Chapter 220 - Brainwashing Aura
chapters.body:
Chapter 220 - Brainwashing Aura

Yin Jiangxue smiled bitterly. "Sorry, my dear."

"You've done nothing wrong." Sheng Guang knew his wife acted very outgoing and carefree, but she was actually a very s.e.n.s.i.t.i.v.e person—especially under the influence of her last marriage, which had caused too much damage. "Our marriage is founded on love and trust, not children. If we did have a kid, we can raise them together. It's fine if we don't have children as well. When we're old and white haired, we can travel the world as husband and wife."

Yin Jiangxue chuckled at his statement. The melancholic emotions also began to dissipate.

She suddenly recalled something. "I heard Shi Sui say little Xiang Yi knows some medical skills. Why don't we ask her to check my pulse?"

Sheng Guang nodded. "Okay, I'll go with you."

The live comments exploded once again.

——[Even if you're desperate for a doctor, you shouldn't be this rash, right?]

——[lmao, the Vase Fairy can't cure every disease…especially something as serious as infertility]

——[The Xiang woman comes with her own brainwashing aura. Anyone that comes within three meters of her will be 100% affected~]

In the kitchen.

Xiang Yi placed the chocolate mousse cake she prepared in the refrigerator so it could set before they ate.

At the same time, the oven made a "ding" sound, indicating that her cupcakes were ready.

Xiang Yi took out a few serving plates and carefully decorated each cake with icing and cherries.

Carrying the cake on top of a serving tray, Xiang Yi brought the dessert into the living room. Everyone's individual cake was normally sweetened, and her second brother's portion had less sugar to taste.

Xiang Li took a bite and his eyebrows curved into a smile.

Bunbun still remembered his preferences.

"Whoa, Little Xiang Yi, you've made cake!" Yin Jiangxue exclaimed as they returned from outside. Her eyes lit up. "Oh my, but I need to diet…"

Xiang Yi: "Then I'll make you one with less sugar?"

Yin Jiangxue waved her hands. "No need, I'll just have one bite!"

She picked up a cupcake and took a bite. The fruit was fresh, the icing creamy, the cake sweet and fragrant. Combined, the texture was soft and delicate to the bite.

Delicious!

With that, one bite turned into many bites…

Yin Jiangxue continued to stuff her mouth. "Little Xiang Yi, you must be a professional dessert chef!"

As a chef, Xiang Yi felt a great sense of accomplishment when someone compliments their cooking. She smiled sheepishly and poured Yin Jiangxue a cup of red tea.

The cake was sweet, so combined with concentrated red tea, the flavor was immediately accentuated.

The next second, she had snatched Sheng Guang's half-eaten cupcake from his hands.

Sheng Guang looked helpless. "Honey, weren't you trying to lose weight?"

"Shh! Be quiet. Don't let my fat hear you! As long as I eat it fast enough, the calories won't catch up!"

Sheng Guang: "…"

——[Hahaha Mentor Yin, the jester of "Two Under One Roof"!]

——[Mr. Sheng Guang's expression is so indulgent, he must really be in love with Yin Jiangxue ]

——[Who doesn't love such a beautiful and interesting Mama Yin~ And she's such a good actress, too]

——[Bleh, so what? Either way, she's a woman in her second marriage, and she can't even have kids]

——[Wow, this person is really an idiot, I suggest everyone report them!]

…

Yin Jiangxue finally stopped after her third cupcake.

She flicked her hair as if nothing happened.

Sheng Guang suddenly remembered. "Oh right, didn't you say you wanted Miss Xiang to take your pulse?"

"Oh, right…" Yin Jiangxue turned to Xiang Yi with an anticipating gaze. "Little XIang Yi, can you check my pulse?"

chapters.title: Chapter 221 - Fancy Shaming (1)
chapters.body:
Chapter 221 - Fancy Shaming (1)

Xiang Yi said, "Of course, are you feeling unwell?"

Yin Jiangxue wasn't wishy-washy either, and she asked truthfully, "I just want to know if there's a possibility of me getting pregnant."

Xiang Yi nodded slightly and said, "Please stretch your left hand toward me."

Yin Jiangxue reached her hand out and placed it in front of Xiang Yi nervously.

After about two minutes, the young girl said in a warm voice, "Now your right hand, please."

The pulse diagnosis method relied on visual observation, hearing, and asking questions. There was a popular saying that men would have their pulse taken with their left hand, while women with their right. However, having the pulse of both hands taken was the only way to obtain a more comprehensive and accurate diagnosis.

After taking the pulse, Xiang Yi frowned slightly and stayed silent for a while.

When Yin Jiangxue saw her expression, her heart sank.

Despite her disappointment, she asked Xiang Yi, "I'm fine, you don't have to think about what to say to comfort me… I know I'm not destined to have children in this life…"

Sheng Guang put his arm around her shoulder and patted it gently.

Xiang Yi looked up in surprise and said, "Miss Yin, you've misunderstood. I was pondering over which prescription is the best."

She had once transmigrated to a world where she studied medicine hard and became the first female imperial physician in order to assist the female protagonist in becoming the empress.

She was best at gynecology.

However, she was too good at it, and her forte actually brought her troubles instead—just like how a star student would be troubled about which one of the numerous solutions they knew to use for answering the question…

Yin Jiangxue was over the moon. "You mean, I can still conceive!?"

"I'll give it a try. There's a 90% chance of success, but the follow-up will require you to cooperate with me to nurse your body," Xiang Yi said conservatively.

'90%'

Yin Jiangxue was in disbelief.

All the doctors she had seen before had all said that there was little hope of her conceiving.

The most optimistic doctor said that she would at most have a 20% chance of success!

Xiang Yi took a pen and paper, wrote down the prescription on the paper, and then handed it back to her. "I'll compile a list of prescriptions and herbal recipes and send it to you soon. If you're still worried, you can take them to an expert and confirm that it's safe for consumption before purchasing some medicine according to the prescription."

Extremely elated as if she had obtained a treasure, Yin Jiangxue thanked her profusely, "Thank you… thank you so much, Xiang Yi! I'll definitely do what you said!"

Among the viewers watching the live broadcast, some were genuinely happy for Yin Jiangxue, while some were skeptical about it.

——[What magical prescription is that? Was she told to just drink more water?]

——[Vase Fairy, please go and read some books about talking tactfully. You're always talking big, I can't stand it.]

——[First, she said she could treat her brother in five treatments, now she said there's a 90% chance that she can help Mama Yin conceive. She even confidently said that her prescription would withstand the test of experts. Does she really think she's a genius doctor?]

——[No, no, no, she's not confident, she just knows others can't hold it against her! After the filming of this program ends, Dr. Xiang and Miss Yin will have nothing to do with her anymore. She's merely doing that on purpose because she knows that they won't hold it against her. Tsk, if only I could hire an expert now, I would definitely embarrass Vase Fairy and prove her wrong on the spot!]

As soon as the pop-up messages floated across the screen, some staff members entered again to whisper a few words to Li Jianyu.

This time, Li Jianyu did not slip into a trance but was, instead, dumbfounded.

'No, it can't be, right?'

The chairman of the Traditional Chinese Medicine Association had requested to be on their variety show with a few powerful figures of the industry! The reason was…

He kept his gaze fixed on Xiang Yi, gulped, and took the microphone.

"Um, Xiang Yi, someone is looking for you…"

"They seem to be a few old men…"

…

chapters.title: Chapter 222 - Fancy Shaming (2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 222 - Fancy Shaming (2)

…

…

Outside the mansion.

Xiang Yi didn't know any of the old men in front of her at all.

Politely, she greeted, "Hello, sirs… are you looking for me?"

The chairman of the Traditional Chinese Medicine Association calmly said, "Yes. Here's the thing, we would like to popularize medical knowledge and promote Traditional Chinese Medicine culture to the public with the help of your program. Would that be convenient?"

Xiang Yi looked at Li Jianyu and said, "Director Li…"

"Of course! It'd be an honor for our program!" said Li Jianyu who was behind the camera and wiping his sweat profusely. He was well aware of the identity of those three big shots!

Mr. Fang, the chairman of the Traditional Chinese Medicine Association of China…

Mr. Yun, the successor of Hui Chun Tang Medicine Hall…

Mr. Jing, chief of the Ji Shi Tang Medicine Hall…

If these three people were characters in immortal novels, they would be the divine old men hidden in the ring of the main character!

It would be no exaggeration to say that they were the saints of the medical world whom even the wealthy and powerful could not invite to appear!

Yet, they requested to spread awareness and medical knowledge in "Two Under One Roof".

The program wasn't worthy of their prestige…

"Please come with me," Xiang Yi said as she took the lead. She couldn't help but take a few more glances at the Elder Yun.

The mild-tempered Elder Yun said with a smile, "Girl, do you find me familiar-looking?"

Xiang Yi nodded and answered, "Yes, there seems to be photos of you in 'Guide To Legendary Traditional Chinese Medicine Recipes'."

"Oh? You actually read the book I wrote? Are you interested in medicinal cuisines?" Elder Yun asked with surprise and joy in his eyes.

At the mention of a past event, the young girl Xiang Yi got a little embarrassed.

"Actually, I just treated them as recipes. At that time, I just learned medicine from my grandfather and I would spend each day memorizing the names of herbs or medicines. I found it too boring, so I started cooking using the recipes in your book… My favorite dish is the American ginseng lean pork soup, which tastes superb with red dates…"

Elder Yun laughed and looked at Elder Fang and Mr. Jing with a smug expression.

'This disciple is mine!'

Elder Fang remained calm and stroked his beard, resembling an immortal-like expert.

On the other hand, Mr. Jing humphed in disdain. "You've written several books, but only the guide to herbal recipes is a best-seller. Publishers must be upset to see you."

Elder Yun was speechless.

In addition to medicinal cuisine therapy, he also did research on other aspects of Chinese medicine and wrote books that were full of complex and profound content. He would often cite ancient writings in his books, too. Not only did it require the reader to be extremely learned in the field of traditional medicine, they had to be cultured as well in order to understand what the book was about.

That resulted in the poor sales of most of his books…

Elder Yun smiled and said, "I heard that you got poisoned while testing some herbs a few days ago. You're so dedicated to your work."

Elder Jing was flabbergasted!

Turning ashamed and angry, he hollered, "That's a foreign herb! I've never seen it before, so I got careless and ended up getting poisoned!"

The live broadcast room was full of boisterous fun.

——[I've found out, these are the giants of the Traditional Chinese Medicine field in our country, but they act like elementary school students with each other hahaha]

——[Master! Teach me to insult others… Ah no, teach me medicine!]

——[Exposing each other's flaws is fine, keep doing so! However, I don't mind hearing more gossip.]

…

As soon as they entered the living room, the first thing they did was exchange pleasantries.

Upon hearing their true identities, Yin Jiangxue's eyes lit up, but they soon lost their l.u.s.ter…

'How can such bigwigs help me so easily?'

chapters.title: Chapter 223 - Fancy Shaming (3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 223 - Fancy Shaming (3)

During her search for famous doctors these years, she had seen all sides to humanity.

She had met doctors who were benevolent, as well as some who were just greedy for money. She even met some con artists who knew little about the field of medicine.

The incredible and excellent doctors were difficult for ordinary people to make an appointment with.

After pondering about it, she sighed and thought that it would be better to let nature take its course. If she could find a few experts to treat her, that would be wonderful but if they couldn't heal her, there was nothing she could do. Well, they didn't owe her a living.

Xiang Yi took out a tea set that her third brother, Xiang Qi, had given to her as a gift. Xiang Qi had a good taste and aesthetic sense. Thus, the tea set he chose had an ancient beauty to it and it was pleasing to the eye.

She warmed the cup, rinsed the tea, and started brewing some tea… Being skillful, her actions were fluid and she had a natural elegance.

After Elder Fang and the others tasted the tea, they looked at Xiang Yi with an even more benevolent gaze, as if they were looking at their disciple.

Elder Jing asked eagerly, "Girl, who did you learn medicine from?"

Xiang Yi answered truthfully, "My grandfather."

"That old man Xiang Yin?" Elder Jing frowned and continued, "His medical skills are not bad…"

He was Xiang Yin's friend on WeChat and thus knew that Xiang Yin often enjoyed showing off about his adorable and lovely granddaughter, which annoyed Elder Jing. He had long wanted to block Xiang Yin's account.

'Now that I think about it, I really regret it! I missed a student with such great potential!'

Elder Yun sipped his tea and said, "It's rare for you to praise anyone. I thought your vocabulary was so limited that you never learned how to give compliments."

Elder Jing retorted, "Complimentary words are used to praise people."

Elder Yun was speechless.

Seeing that his two old friends were about to bicker again, Elder Fang pinched his temples and interjected, "Enough, don't forget what we're here for."

She subconsciously retreated backwards to hide behind her brother.

While looking down and sipping tea, Xiang Li smirked.

However, he seemed to have seen through something and removed his glasses with a smirk. "What form of medical knowledge are you planning to promote? Our production crew will cooperate fully."

Elder Fang said calmly, "If it's purely in terms of theory, I reckon it would be too dry and boring. The young audience don't like listening to us talk about the five elements of yin and yang and etc. either. We're planning to convey it through experiment. Is anyone here suitable for us to practice on? For example someone with a cold or a headache."

At first, no one dared to speak out of consideration of their identities.

It was rather silent at the scene.

Li Jianyu was the first to raise his hand and speak. "I… I developed some oral and stomach ulcers lately. Can that be cured?"

Elder Yun suggested that he follow a specific diet that is rich in fruits like pears, loquat, and tomatoes.

On the other hand, Elder Jing gave some prescriptions.

With Li Jianyu taking the lead, other staff members raised their hands.

Yin Jiangxue who was at the side, was a little hesitant.

If she caused the experts to be criticized by the netizens…

She didn't want to see that happen.

Sheng Guang held her hand and said, "Madam, why don't we show the prescriptions to these few masters?"

Yin Jiangxue sighed with relief and said, "That's good."

If the masters approved of Xiang Yi's prescription, she would get some medicine based on it.

At the thought of this, Yin Jiangxue handed a prescription to him.

Due to the fact that she had folded it and the quality of the paper was similar, she didn't realize that she had handed over the prescription given to her by the previous doctor…

chapters.title: Chapter 224 - Fancy Shaming (4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 224 - Fancy Shaming (4)

Elder Fang took her prescription.

The three of them first took Yin Jiangxue's pulse and then glanced at the prescription before furrowing his brows.

He could tell that the prescription was not written by Xiang Yi.

He passed the prescription to Elder Yun.

Yin Jiangxue asked apprehensively, "Elder Fang, is it because of my body?"

After reading it, Elder Yun had a similar reaction as Elder Fang and passed it to Elder Jing, who was the most proficient in pharmacology

The more Elder Jing looked at it, the angrier he got. He was hot-tempered to begin with and thus, he smacked the table, causing some tea droplets to splatter onto the table.

"Nonsense! What is this stupid prescription! It looks like a bunch of expensive tonic products, but in fact it's just a nonsensical acc.u.mulation of random ingredients! It only helps with the symptoms and doesn't cure the root of the illness. This prescription is going to damage one's body too!"

"If you consume it in the long term, you won't be able to conceive and even your internal organs and meridians will be affected! The person who wrote this prescription does not deserve to be a doctor!"

Yin Jiangxue's face turned pale. 'So I can't conceive…'

The netizens in the livestream room were enraged.

—— [Damn it, I want to throw a gas can at her! She's out to kill someone!]

—— [Oh my God, she looks so kind and polite on the surface when she addresses her as 'Miss Yin', I didn't expect her to be such a hypocrite! If you don't have a cure for it, why do you have to hurt others?!?!]

—— [Ah, big shots, come and look at her brother's hand, could his condition have deteriorated too? I'm breaking out in cold sweat!!!]

——[Damn, right, she performed acupuncture on Dr. Xiang too! He mentioned a burning sensation. Could it have damaged his nerve? There shouldn't be a burning sensation during normal acupuncture!]

…

Many netizens were cursing their hearts out in the comments section.

During the key juncture, Shi Sui squinted and asked in a firm tone, "Mama Yin, did Xiang Yi give you that prescription?"ue

As soon as she said that, there was yet another uproar in the comments section. [Why is Shi Sui always defending that Vase Fairy? She was clearly the one who wrote that prescription. Does he really think the ten million viewers of the livestream are blind!?!]

Yin Jiangxue was stunned but she hurriedly rummaged through her pocket, only to realize that she had another prescription!

"I'm sorry, but this prescription is the one that Miss Xiang Yi gave me… Sorry to trouble you…"

'Oh?'

'This is a prescription from the little divine doctor?'

Elder Jing was the first to snatch it and took a careful look at it.

The more he looked at it, the more his eyes glistened!

'What a superb prescription!'

However, if he perfected the prescription, it would be even better!

Elder Yun said eagerly, "Let me take a look."

Elder Jing turned around childishly and exclaimed, "No! It's mine!"

Elder Yun complained to Elder Fang, "Chairman, look, he's so tyrannical!"

Elder Fang was a little speechless. He coughed and gestured for Elder Jing to not go overboard.

Elder Jing shifted the prescription towards Elder Yun reluctantly and said, "Let's go and take a look."

Elder Yun put on his presbyopic glasses and the two of them whose ages added up to more than a hundred years old, praised incessantly while reading the prescription, as if they were the rating system of a music game.

"Her handwriting is as elegant as the breeze."

"The combination of these medicines together is novel and unique."

"This potion is very mild and it's the best for nourishing one's body."

They smacked their t.h.i.g.hs in unison and exclaimed, "Wonderful!"

chapters.title: Chapter 225 - 225: Go Ahead If You Can Do It
chapters.body:
